REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF JACKSON TOWER ASSOCIATES, L.P.S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT?S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 65858649) FILED BY PLAINTIFF JACKSON TOWER ASSOCIATES, L.P.
REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER (TRANSACTION ID # 65935286) FILED BY PLAINTIFF JACKSON TOWER ASSOCIATES, L.P.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT HANA KAWAGUCHI DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY STATES FACTS TO WITHSTAND A DEMURRER. THE COURT NOTES THAT CONTRARY TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM THAT THIS ACTION IS SOLELY/PRIMARILY BASED ON BREACH OF CONTRACT, IT IS NOT. PLAINTIFF EXPRESSLY SEEKS A DETERMINATION WHETHER DEFENDANT PERMANENTLY RESIDES AT THE PREMISES, A DETERMINATION TO BE MADE BY THE RENT BOARD. HOWEVER, OTHER RELIEF SOUGHT MAY NOT BE ADDRESSED AT THE RENT BOARD, AND THEREFORE, THIS CASE IS PROPERLY BEFORE THIS COURT AS A WHOLE. FURTHERMORE, ANY DETERMINATIONS AS TO THE LEGALITY OF THE LEASE PROVISIONS AND WHETHER VIOLATIONS OF THOSE PROVISIONS CONSTITUTE BREACH OF CONTRACT, IS AN ISSUE OF FACT TO BE DETERMINED LATER IN THE LITIGATION. ORDER FOR THE SAME UNDER REVIEW. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-14-2020 Set For Trial On May-24-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Oct-28-2020 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Courtcall:
Justin Goodman, Esq., 415-956-8100, appearing for Jackson Tower, Plaintiff
No other appearances

During the course of calling attendance, Mr. Goodman stated that he does not contest to the tentative ruling and that he submitted a proposed form of order that tracks the tenative ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HANA KAWAGUCHI DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, complaint sufficiently states facts to withstand a demurrer. The Court notes that contrary to Plaintiff's claim that this action is solely/primarily based on breach of contract, it is not. Plaintiff expressly seeks a determination whether defendant permanently resides at the premises, a determination to be made by the Rent Board. However, other relief sought may not be addressed at the Rent Board, and therefore, this case is properly before this Court as a whole. Furthermore, any determinations as to the legality of the lease provisions and whether violations of those provisions constitute breach of contract, is an issue of fact to be determined later in the litigation. Order for the same under review. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-31-2020 Continued To Oct-01-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HANA KAWAGUCHI DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is continued to October 1, 2020. By no later than September 15, 2020 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief addressing arguments raised in reply. Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
